task_completion.description: OpenRocket is running with a rocket design loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket GUI is visible on the screen with a rocket design displayed in the main view.

task_completion.description: The required mass components ('Egg', 'Altimeter', 'Tuning Ballast') are present in the rocket design.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenRocket component tree, there are mass components named 'Egg', 'Altimeter', and 'Tuning Ballast' located in the forward section of the rocket.

task_completion.description: The flight simulation achieves the target apogee and ground hit velocity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket flight simulations tab shows a simulation run with an apogee between 256.0 m and 262.0 m, and a ground hit velocity of 4.5 m/s or less.

task_completion.description: The rocket design is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket window title shows 'tarc_competition_design.ork' or a save dialog is visible saving the file to '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/tarc_competition_design.ork'.

task_completion.description: The engineering notebook report is created with the required details.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing the file 'tarc_engineering_notebook.md' containing documented values for body tube diameter (>= 46 mm), motor selected, tuning ballast mass, apogee (~259 m), and ground hit velocity (<= 4.5 m/s).

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the design and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenRocket GUI being actively used (e.g., component edit dialogs, simulation tabs) rather than just a terminal or text editor modifying the .ork XML file.

integrity.description: The simulation results in OpenRocket appear genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The flight simulations tab in OpenRocket shows populated data rows with realistic values for velocity, altitude, and flight time, indicating a real simulation was executed.

integrity.description: The values in the report match the values seen in the OpenRocket simulation results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The apogee, ground hit velocity, and other parameters documented in the markdown report correspond to the actual simulation results visible in the OpenRocket GUI screenshots.
